title: grevillea juncifolia subsp. temulenta
intro: Grevillea juncifolia subsp. temulenta, also known as the prickly spider flower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Australian indigenous medicine to treat various health conditions. The prickly spider flower is believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or infused oil to be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
